evals.prompt: Start a MiR robot I can test against, then tell me its battery level and whether it has any errors.
evals.expected_output: An emulator is started (uv run mir-emulator), GET /status is called with correct MiR Basic auth (SHA-256-hashed password), and battery_percentage plus errors are reported in plain language.

evals.prompt: Queue up whatever charging-related mission the robot on localhost:8080 has, wait for it to finish, and confirm it completed.
evals.expected_output: GET /missions is used to find a mission whose name matches charging (no guessed guids), POST /mission_queue enqueues it, GET /mission_queue/{id} is polled until state is Done, and completion is reported from observed state.

evals.prompt: Simulate an emergency stop on the emulator, show me what the fleet sees for that robot, then recover it.
evals.expected_output: PUT /_emulator/faults activates emergency_stop, the fleet view (GET /api/v1/robots with x-api-key) shows the Emergency Stop end state, and recovery clears the fault via /_emulator/faults (noting clear_error cannot clear an e-stop, matching MiR).
